name: Sermorelin
subtitle: GHRH Analog | Growth Hormone Releasing Hormone
overview: Sermorelin is a synthetic 29-amino acid analog of human growth hormone-releasing hormone that stimulates natural growth hormone production while preserving physiological pulsatile patterns. Originally FDA-approved in 1997 for pediatric GH deficiency, it was discontinued in 2008 for manufacturing reasons, not safety concerns.
mechanism: Subcutaneous injection provides optimal bioavailability for binding GHRH receptors, stimulating pulsatile GH release while maintaining hypothalamic-pituitary axis integrity and allowing natural somatostatin negative feedback.
keyBenefits: 1.26kg lean mass increase documented in elderly

items.name: Lean Body Mass Enhancement
items.description: 1.26kg lean mass increase documented in elderly men with improved muscle strength tests.

items.name: IGF-1 Mediated Growth
items.description: Endogenous IGF-1 stimulation drives protein synthesis and muscle growth.

items.name: Athletic Recovery
items.description: Enhanced recovery through physiological GH stimulation.

items.name: Age-Related GH Decline Reversal
items.description: Doubles 12-hour GH release in elderly subjects over 6 weeks.

items.name: Body Composition Improvement
items.description: Decreased adiposity and improved lean mass distribution.

items.name: Endogenous GH Stimulation
items.description: Preserves natural axis function without suppression.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary method. Bedtime administration aligns with natural nocturnal GH pulses. Avoid food 2+ hours before injection as carbohydrates blunt GH response.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Clean vial top with alcohol swab; allow to dry
Inject 3mL bacteriostatic water slowly against vial wall (not directly on powder)
Gently swirl in circular motion - do not shake vigorously
Allow 2-3 minutes until powder dissolves into clear solution
Store reconstituted solution at 2-8°C; use within 10-30 days
Inject subcutaneously at 45-degree angle; rotate sites

keyBenefits: Lower abdomen (avoid navel), anterior thigh, posterior upper arm

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal spray is available with 69% patient preference over injection. Lower bioavailability but bypasses first-pass metabolism.

interactions.notes: Excellent combination producing 3-5 fold increases in GH release.
Highly effective combination - CJC-1295 provides sustained 6-8 day release while sermorelin offers immediate pulsatile effects.
Combined GHRH+GHRP-2 produces 54-fold GH increases compared to 20-fold with GHRH alone.
Somatostatin analogs directly block GH release by activating inhibitory receptors.
High-dose glucocorticoids suppress pituitary GH release and reduce GHRH receptor sensitivity.
GH antagonizes insulin action; monitor combination closely.
Essential combination as untreated hypothyroidism prevents sermorelin response.

timeline.effects: IGF-1 elevation begins; improved sleep quality and recovery
Body composition changes begin; increased energy and well-being
Visible muscle tone improvements; fat reduction; skin quality enhancement
Maximum benefits: muscle growth, fat loss, anti-aging effects

common: Injection site reactions (16.7% of patients - generally mild)
stopSigns: Signs of pituitary tumor growth (headaches, vision changes)
Severe injection site reactions or generalized allergic responses
Uncontrolled diabetes or significant glucose intolerance
New onset or worsening malignancy symptoms
monitoring: Monitor thyroid function - 6.5% develop hypothyroidism requiring hormone replacement
Check IGF-1 levels monthly initially, then every 3-6 months long-term

good: Completely clear and colorless solution without particles, cloudiness, or precipitation
Greater than 98% peptide purity per USP standards
Produced in FDA-registered facilities following cGMP
warning: Brief room temperature exposure acceptable up to 72 hours but should be refrigerated promptly
bad: Any cloudiness, particles, color changes, or precipitation indicates degradation
Molecular weight should be exactly 3,358 daltons (free base) or 3,418 daltons (acetate salt)

references.title: The effects of intranasal insufflation of growth hormone releasing factor analogue GRF 1-29 NH2 on growth hormone secretion in children with short stature
references.keyFindings: Intranasal GRF 1-29 at 100mcg/kg induced prompt GH release in prepubertal children with peak values at 15 minutes. Demonstrated feasibility of nasal delivery with minimal side effects.

references.title: Growth hormone (GH)-releasing hormone-(1-29) twice daily reverses the decreased GH and insulin-like growth factor-I levels in old men
references.keyFindings: Landmark study in healthy old men (68 +/- 6.2 yr). High-dose GHRH(1-29) twice daily for 14 days reversed age-related decreases in GH and IGF-I to levels not significantly different from young men.

references.title: Once daily subcutaneous growth hormone-releasing hormone therapy accelerates growth in growth hormone-deficient children during the first year of therapy
references.keyFindings: Pivotal multicenter trial with 110 prepubertal GH-deficient children. Sermorelin 30mcg/kg/day at bedtime increased height velocity from 4.1 to 8.0 cm/year at 6 months, with 74% achieving good response.

references.title: Endocrine and metabolic effects of long-term administration of [Nle27]growth hormone-releasing hormone-(1-29)-NH2 in age-advanced men and women
references.keyFindings: 5-month single-blind RCT in 19 adults (55-71 yr). Nightly GHRH analog for 4 months activated somatotropic axis. Men showed increased lean body mass, insulin sensitivity, and well-being. Skin thickness improved in both genders.

references.title: Sermorelin: a better approach to management of adult-onset growth hormone insufficiency?
references.keyFindings: Review arguing sermorelin preserves pituitary function and physiological GH pulsatility, offering a safer alternative to exogenous GH for adult-onset GH insufficiency.

latestResearch.title: Growth hormone-releasing hormone and its analogues in health and disease
latestResearch.summary: Major review covering GHRH and analogs across oncology, regenerative medicine, metabolic disorders, and neuroprotection. Supports therapeutic potential of GHRH agonists as clinical therapeutics.

quickStats.typicalDose: 200-300mcg per dose (up to 500mcg for athletic performance)
quickStats.frequency: Once daily at bedtime (aligns with natural GH pulse)
